Ref: {{ref}}
Lender Interview Questions
SBA 7(a) ESOP Qualification
Lender
{{lenderName}}
Company
{{companyName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Trustee Relationship

ESOP transactions require an independent trustee to represent plan participants. The lender’s ability to work constructively with the trustee is critical to closing.

A.Have you closed SBA 7(a) loans where an independent ESOP trustee was involved? How many in the last 36 months?
B.What is your comfort level with the trustee having approval rights over the purchase price and transaction structure?
C.Do you have a preferred list of trustee firms you have worked with, or do you accept any nationally recognized independent trustee?
D.How do you typically handle the trustee’s fairness opinion or FMV appraisal in your underwriting process?
E.Are you comfortable sharing your term sheet and underwriting assumptions directly with the trustee?
§ 02Seller Personal Guarantee

SBA SOPs require personal guarantees, but ESOP transactions present unique PG considerations since the seller is exiting and the ESOP trust is the borrower.

A.What are your standard personal guarantee requirements for SBA 7(a) acquisition loans?
B.Are you willing to structure a seller PG that sunsets after a defined period (e.g., 24 months) contingent on DSCR performance?
C.Do you require PGs from all officers/managers, or only the selling owner? How do you handle PGs when the post-close management team changes?
D.For ESOP transactions where the seller is fully exiting, do you offer any PG relief based on the ESOP trust’s assets or the company’s operating history?
E.What collateral do you require beyond the PG? Are you comfortable with the business assets and ESOP trust as primary collateral?
§ 03Stretch Capacity

Many ESOP deals require financing above the SBA 7(a) standard limit. Lender participation and intercreditor arrangements are often necessary.

A.What is your maximum SBA 7(a) loan amount? Can you accommodate deals that require stretch financing above $5M?
B.Do you participate with other SBA lenders for above-limit deals, or do you prefer a single-lender structure with a seller note subordination?
C.Have you executed intercreditor agreements with subordinated seller note holders in SBA transactions? What terms do you typically require?
D.What DSCR threshold do you require to release the seller note from subordination?
E.Are you comfortable with a dual-lender structure (SBA + conventional) if the deal size exceeds your SBA limit?
§ 04Timeline Commitment

ESOP transactions operate on a defined timeline. Lender responsiveness directly impacts the 120-day closing target.

A.What is your typical turnaround time for a term sheet after receiving a complete application package? Our target is 14 business days.
B.Once the term sheet is issued, what is your average underwriting timeline from LOI to closing?
C.Do you have dedicated ESOP underwriting staff, or would this be handled by your general SBA team?
D.How do you handle concurrent workstreams — can you underwrite while the trustee appraisal and ERISA counsel review are in progress?
E.What documents do you require at application vs. at underwriting? Can we stage submissions to avoid bottlenecks?
§ 05ESOP Experience

ESOP acquisitions have distinct structures, regulatory requirements, and risk profiles compared to standard business acquisitions.

A.How many ESOP acquisition loans has your institution closed in the last 5 years? What was the average deal size?
B.What is your understanding of ERISA fiduciary requirements as they relate to ESOP financing?
C.Are you familiar with IRC §1042 tax-deferred sales and how the Qualified Replacement Securities requirement affects the seller’s post-close liquidity?
D.How do you evaluate repurchase obligation risk in your underwriting? Do you require a repurchase liability study?
E.Are you comfortable with the seller note being subordinated to the SBA loan for the first 2–3 years, with interest accruing but not paid during subordination?
§ 06Process & Communication

Clear communication channels and defined processes reduce friction during the transaction.

A.Who will be our dedicated point of contact throughout the underwriting and closing process?
B.What is your preferred communication cadence — weekly status calls, email updates, or both?
C.Do you provide a structured document checklist at term sheet stage, or do requests come ad hoc during underwriting?
D.How do you coordinate with the seller’s CPA, ERISA counsel, and the independent trustee during due diligence?
E.What is your policy on deal team continuity — will the same team follow the deal from term sheet through closing and servicing?
§ 07Scoring Rubric

Responses to these questions are scored against our Lender Scoring Rubric (item #25). The rubric evaluates lenders across six dimensions: ESOP experience, timeline reliability, structural flexibility, communication quality, stretch capacity, and cost competitiveness.

Scoring: Each question is weighted. Lenders scoring below the threshold on any dimension may not advance to term sheet stage. The rubric is shared with the seller and trustee as part of the lender recommendation.
§ 08Contact

For questions about this document, please contact:
